strengths: 17+ years of verifiable software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains — meets the minimum experience threshold
Founder of GISCollective (2016–present), a multi-year open-source geo-data platform serving real organizations — demonstrates entrepreneurial execution beyond employment
Current role at re:cinq includes hands-on AI-agent infrastructure: MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — shows directional pivot toward AI tooling
concerns: AI expertise is shallow and very recent — no demonstrated AI products or platforms of scale, no ML background, no recognized standing in the AI field prior to 2025
No evidence of director-level IC scope: no history of advising strategic customers on AI/cloud adoption, shaping product strategy, or influencing across organizational boundaries
Education is two bachelor's degrees (CS and Fine Arts); no Master's or PhD in an AI-related field — preferred qualification entirely absent
response.justification: The role's core competencies are recognized AI expertise, director-level strategic influence, and the ability to advise customers and partners on AI adoption — areas where this resume provides almost no evidence. The candidate's AI involvement is limited to one line in a role started 14 months ago (MCP tooling at re:cinq), with no prior AI or ML work, no AI-focused publications or products, and no history of operating at a strategic advisory layer. The GISCollective founder credit and breadth of engineering experience are genuine positives, and the 17-year tenure meets the minimum bar, but the seniority gap (Staff Engineer vs. director-level IC) and absence of any demonstrated AI thought leadership make this candidate a poor fit for the role as defined.
